meaning_english: Meaning: To restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something. Opposite: Indulge, Consume, Partake, Embrace
Meaning: To warn or reprimand someone firmly. Opposite: Praise, Applaud, Commend, Extol
Meaning: To make something bad better. Opposite: Deteriorate, Worsen, Exacerbate, Aggravate
Meaning: Strong hostility or ill will. Opposite: Goodwill, Amity, Benevolence, Friendship
Meaning: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern. Opposite: Enthusiasm, Zeal, Eagerness, Fervor
Meaning: Very old or old-fashioned. Opposite: Modern, Contemporary, Current, Trendy
Meaning: To make an unpleasant feeling less intense. Opposite: Exasperate, Provoke, Intensify, Agitate
Meaning: Showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks. Opposite: Timid, Meek, Cowardly, Fearful
Meaning: Hostile and aggressive. Opposite: Peaceful, Pacific, Conciliatory, Friendly
Meaning: To scold or rebuke. Opposite: Flatter, Commend, Praise, Approve
Meaning: Having knowledge or being aware of. Opposite: Ignorant, Unaware, Unconscious, Oblivious
Meaning: Showing uncritical satisfaction with oneself. Opposite: Humble, Discontented, Anxious, Dissatisfied
Meaning: Standing out so as to be clearly visible. Opposite: Hidden, Obscure, Inconspicuous, Camouflaged
Meaning: To reduce in extent or quantity. Opposite: Prolong, Extend, Lengthen, Expand
Meaning: To put off to a later time; postpone. Opposite: Hasten, Expedite, Accelerate, Advance
Meaning: The complete loss or absence of hope. Opposite: Hope, Optimism, Confidence, Elation
Meaning: Ready to accept control or instruction; submissive. Opposite: Stubborn, Unruly, Rebellious, Defiant
Meaning: Ecstatically happy. Opposite: Depressed, Miserable, Dejected, Sorrowful
Meaning: A person or thing that is mysterious or difficult to understand. Opposite: Clarity, Solution, Explanation, Directness
Meaning: A mass departure of people. Opposite: Influx, Arrival, Entrance, Homecoming
Meaning: Having or displaying a passionate intensity. Opposite: Apathetic, Cool, Indifferent, Emotionless
Meaning: Easily persuaded to believe something. Opposite: Skeptical, Astute, Suspicious, Shrewd
Meaning: To create difficulties for someone or something. Opposite: Aid, Facilitate, Support, Assist
Meaning: Suggested though not directly expressed. Opposite: Explicit, Clear, Direct, Stated
Meaning: Formally accuse of or charge with a serious crime. Opposite: Exonerate, Acquit, Absolve, Pardon
Meaning: Inborn; natural. Opposite: Acquired, Learned, Extrinsic, Cultivated
Meaning: To feel intense dislike or disgust for. Opposite: Adore, Love, Admire, Cherish
Meaning: To make less severe, serious, or painful. Opposite: Intensify, Enhance, Exacerbate, Increase
Meaning: Not discovered or known about; uncertain. Opposite: Lucid, Clear, Obvious, Transparent
Meaning: Acting with or showing care and thought for the future. Opposite: Imprudent, Foolish, Reckless, Rash
Meaning: Strong and healthy; vigorous. Opposite: Frail, Weak, Feeble, Delicate
Meaning: Slow-moving or inactive. Opposite: Brisk, Active, Energetic, Swift
Meaning: Of little value or importance. Opposite: Significant, Vital, Crucial, Essential
Meaning: Not changing in form or character; consistent. Opposite: Varied, Diverse, Inconsistent, Irregular
Meaning: To regard with great respect; revere. Opposite: Despise, Dishonor, Condemn, Disrespect
